verdict.summary: Round 3 resolves the prior major finding in scripts/usage-alert.py. Delivery now checks issue-list and create/edit return codes, retries failed verified-private delivery on the registry using a freshly rendered generic body, returns nonzero when both routes fail, and avoids claiming the maintainer was alerted unless a write succeeds. The added end-to-end tests cover private lookup, create, and edit failures; successful redacted public fallback; both-route failure; and recovery-side lookup failure. The broader changes remain fail-closed: detailed PAT, usage, and model-health data is emitted only after a token-scoped private-repository confirmation, same-repository and indeterminate routes redact, runtime private-route failures do not reuse detailed bodies on the public fallback, and public PAT stdout is coarse. I found no remaining blocker or major issue in the diff.
